content: @Boqun Feng @Miguel Ojeda is it kosher to send a rebase of https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/pull/1036 to the mailing list? I've done it locally. I guess I'd need to "From: Wedson ..", right?
The "From:" should be added automatically by Git when doing format-patch if the Git commit author is correct (i.e. Wedson).
As for whether it is OK or not, Wedson gave us his OK for others to submit his code, so that is fine. Thanks for asking!
Yeah, however, last time when the patch posted, there was some provenance issue: https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/CAH5fLggxsewmtzXjehbawDCTHO0C7kteU_CLnh80eMNj=QyP9Q@mail.gmail.com/

Thanks for pointing that out! So the concern is that as_with_ref throws away the write provenance, which is required for impl<T: ?Sized> From<&WithRef<T>> for Arc<T>. ArcBorrow doesn't have this problem because it holds onto the pointer. Wrapping in UnsafeCell was suggested as a solution, but feels heavy.

Question: do we need impl<T: ?Sized> From<&WithRef<T>> for Arc<T>? It's used by ListArc::try_from_arc_borrow, but that method has no users.

More broadly: do we actually need the ability to turn WithRef (or ArcBorrow) back into an Arc?

For your last question, ArcBorrow is just a "borrow" of an object in Arc, you can get an Arc from ArcBorrow by deref() + .into(), which means increase the reference count (i.e. the callers hold the refcount themselves). This is probably only reasonable ArcBorrow -> Arc "conversion". And I'm not sure getting rid of the deref() part can provide any benefit.
Not sure I understand the answer. The question was: do we need the ability to go ArcBorrow -> Arc? If we can remove it, then the provenance problem vanishes.
Oh I see. I think we need that, the user of ArcBorrow may still want to get their own refcount.
e.g. in fast path, it just a quick check, but in the slow path, the user may get a reference count and send it to something like a workqueue.
if you would like to dig, the interesting question is whether we need ArcBorrow at all, can't we just use &Arc? Compiler should be smart enough to avoid another level of indirection ;-)
Yeah that's a good point. I don't suppose the introduction came with evidence :)
I believe we used to have some godbolt example that shows the differences (i.e. using &Arc introduce an extra level of indirect, a pointer to a pointer), but things could be changed.
Was that godbolt example ever in a PR or on the mailing list?
If you give up the -> Arc conversion, then it's just a fancy &T.
The ArcBorrow type is used in the linked list iterator, and that one can't be replaced with &Arc<T>.

It looks like the iterator currently gets its hands on *T, then *ArcInner<T>, and from there constructs the ArcBorrow; you're saying you can't replace it with &Arc<T> because you just can't construct that, right?
